mitre_attack: T1557.001 - LLMNR/NBT-NS Poisoning and SMB Relay
cwe: CWE-297 - Improper Validation of Certificate with Host Mismatch
CWE-599 - Missing Validation of OpenSSL Certificate

threat.title: Mobile API SSL Pinning Bypass via Runtime Manipulation
threat.description: Mobile applications that fail to implement certificate pinning or use weak pinning implementations are vulnerable to Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attacks. Attackers leverage dynamic instrumentation frameworks like Frida, Objection, or SSL Kill Switch to bypass SSL/TLS certificate validation at runtime, enabling interception of encrypted API communications. This exposes sensitive data including authentication tokens, personal information, financial transactions, and API keys. The attack is particularly dangerous because it can be executed on non-rooted devices using tools like objection, making it accessible to a wider range of attackers.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker sets up a malicious Wi-Fi access point (Evil Twin) or positions themselves on the same network as the victim
2. Attacker installs mitmproxy or Burp Suite to intercept HTTPS traffic and generates a CA certificate
3. On the target mobile device, attacker installs Frida server (or uses objection gadget for non-rooted devices)
4. Attacker runs Frida script to hook SSL/TLS validation functions: frida -U -f com.company.app -l ssl-pinning-bypass.js --no-pause
5. The Frida script bypasses certificate validation by hooking Java/Kotlin (Android) or Swift/Objective-C (iOS) SSL functions
6. All HTTPS traffic now flows through the attacker's proxy, fully decrypted and modifiable
7. Attacker intercepts API requests containing JWT tokens, session IDs, user credentials, payment information, or personal data
8. Attacker can modify requests/responses in real-time, replay tokens, or exfiltrate sensitive data
prerequisites: Mobile application lacks certificate pinning implementation
Application uses weak pinning that can be bypassed with common Frida scripts
No runtime integrity checks or Frida detection mechanisms
No root/jailbreak detection or ineffective detection that can be bypassed
Attacker has physical or network access to the device
Device allows installation of proxy certificates (user trust store)

cve_references.description: Missing SSL Certificate Validation in Indian Bank IndSMART Android App - CWE-295
Apple iOS SSL/TLS goto fail vulnerability allowing MITM attacks due to improper certificate validation
Improper Certificate Validation - Foundation weakness enabling MITM attacks on mobile applications

real_world_breaches.incident: Indian Bank IndSMART Android App SSL Validation Bypass
real_world_breaches.impact: Banking credentials and financial transactions exposed to MITM attacks due to missing SSL certificate validation
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Application accepted self-signed certificates, failed to validate certificate chain, CWE-295 vulnerability

real_world_breaches.impact: All iOS and OS X HTTPS connections vulnerable to MITM attacks for 18 months; affected iOS 6.0-7.0.6
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Duplicate goto statement in SSL/TLS validation code bypassed certificate verification entirely

real_world_breaches.incident: Certificate Validation Study - 40 Popular iOS Apps
real_world_breaches.impact: Only 10 out of 40 popular iOS applications performed proper SSL/TLS validation
real_world_breaches.root_cause: 75% of tested apps lacked certificate pinning and proper validation mechanisms

real_world_breaches.impact: Security researchers demonstrated payment amount manipulation (paying $10 instead of $100) through SSL pinning bypass
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Lack of SSL pinning allowed API request modification during payment transactions

bug_bounty_reports.summary: SSL pinning bypass in mobile banking app allowed interception of account balances, transaction history, and authentication tokens
Missing certificate pinning in healthcare app exposed patient PHI and medical records via MITM attack

security_research.title: OWASP Mobile Top 10 2024 - M5: Insecure Communication
security_research.key_finding: Mobile applications frequently fail to protect network traffic, using SSL/TLS inconsistently or accepting any certificate without validation

security_research.title: SSL Pinning Bypass with Frida and Effective Mitigation Techniques
security_research.key_finding: For most mobile applications, certificate pinning can be bypassed within seconds using Frida if the app uses standard API functions

security_research.title: certmitm: Automatic Exploitation of TLS Certificate Validation Vulnerabilities
security_research.key_finding: Automated tool demonstrating widespread TLS certificate validation vulnerabilities in mobile applications at DEF CON 31

mitigations.title: Implement iOS SSL Pinning with TrustKit
mitigations.description: Deploy certificate pinning on iOS applications using TrustKit framework to validate the server's public key against a known set of certificates. TrustKit supports both Swift and Objective-C and provides backup pins for certificate rotation.
iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
validation_steps: Extract SHA-256 hash of server public key using OpenSSL
Configure TrustKit with primary and backup certificate pins
Test with self-signed certificate via proxy (should fail)
Verify pinning failures are reported to monitoring endpoint

indicators: Unusual SSL/TLS handshake failures or certificate errors in logs
API requests originating from known proxy tools
Multiple failed certificate validation attempts from same device
Requests from devices with mismatched device fingerprints

log_queries.query: SigninLogs | union AppServiceHTTPLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h) | where UserAgent contains "Mobile" | extend IsCertError = (ResultDescription contains "SSL" or ResultDescription contains "certificate") | where IsCertError == true | summarize FailureCount = count() by UserPrincipalName, IPAddress | where FailureCount > 5

breach_evidence.evidence: Indian Bank breach, iOS goto fail, 75% apps lacking validation

expert_review.evidence: OWASP Mobile Top 10, DEF CON 31 research

code_testing.evidence: Production code for TrustKit, Network Security Config
